title: phoradendron krameri
intro: Phoradendron krameri, also known as Khat or False Khat, is a shrub native to East Africa and the Middle East, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. The plant contains cathinone, a stimulant compound that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in Somalia and Ethiopia. Traditionally, the plant's leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, fatigue, and respiratory problems, and are believed to possess medicinal properties such as antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The leaves can be consumed as tea, smoked, or ingested as a powder, and have been used in herbal preparations to promote alertness, relieve stress, and improve cognitive function.
